educational_content.presentation_title: Validity Evidence for the Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery (FLS) Program as an Assessment Tool: A Systematic Review
educational_content.content_summary: Academic presentation reviewing validity evidence for FLS assessment using Messick's validity framework including content, response process, internal structure, relations to other variables, and consequences. Systematic review of 11,628 initial studies narrowed to 23 studies with 1,280 participants. Findings show gaps in validity evidence framework with evidence skewed towards relations with other variables (RoV). Suggests reconsideration of the ligating loop task value.
key_findings: Evidence predominantly supports relations with other variables
Limited evidence for content, response process, and consequences aspects
Questions raised about ligating loop task validity
